text.title: Growth Copywriting Fundamentals Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Growth Copywriting Fundamentals methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each headline writes ≥10 variants. First variant is rarely best; iteration is the lever.
rule.rationale: Each headline writes ≥10 variants. First variant is rarely best; iteration is the lever.

rule.statement: Copy uses VoC excerpts verbatim (≥3 phrases). Inventing language without VoC = guessing.
rule.rationale: Copy uses VoC excerpts verbatim (≥3 phrases). Inventing language without VoC = guessing.

rule.statement: Every feature translates to a named benefit (outcome + emotion). 'Includes X' is forbidden as a benefit.
rule.rationale: Every feature translates to a named benefit (outcome + emotion). 'Includes X' is forbidden as a benefit.

rule.statement: Each section drives ONE CTA. Multiple CTAs reduce conversion (decision fatigue).
rule.rationale: Each section drives ONE CTA. Multiple CTAs reduce conversion (decision fatigue).

rule.statement: Spec names the founder/copy lead. 'team' / 'we' / '' forbidden.
rule.rationale: Spec names the founder/copy lead. 'team' / 'we' / '' forbidden.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-growth-copywriting-fundamentals.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
